revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:行幸]][[zh:行幸]]
[[File:Gyoukou.JPG|thumb|right|240px|Monument commemorating Emperor Showa’s {{ill|Gyōkō|ja|行幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkō|1=Imperial visits|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626055}} (Hiroshima Prefecture, Fukuyama City)]] [[File:第18回全日本都市対抗野球大会開幕式.jpg|thumb|240px|Emperor Showa, Empress Kojun, and Prince Takahito Mikasa attending the opening ceremony of the {{ill|18th National Inter-City Baseball Tournament|ja|第18回都市対抗野球大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/18th National Inter-City Baseball Tournament|qq=}} (August 3, 1947)]]
'''Gyōkō''' (行幸, ぎょうこう, みゆき) refers to an outing by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. If the journey involves multiple destinations, it is specifically called a '''Junkō''' (巡幸).<ref>{{Kotobank|行幸|2=精選版 日本国語大辞典}}</ref>
The term '''{{ill|Gokō|ja|御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokō|1=gokou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16280183}}''' (ごこう, ぎょこう, みゆき) is also used and applies to outings by the {{ill|Daijō Tennō|en|Daijō Tennō|qid=Q14303973|lt=Retired Emperor|1=Daijō Tennō}}, {{ill|Cloistered Emperor|ja|法皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cloistered Emperor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太上法皇|1=Cloistered Emperor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3053200}}, or {{ill|Imperial Consort Dowager|ja|女院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Consort Dowager|1=nyoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10075498}}.
== Usage == The outings of the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|qid=Q7409248|lt=Empress Dowager|1=Empress dowager}}, {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince|1=Crown prince}}, or {{ill|Crown Princess|ja|皇太子妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Princess|1=Crown Princess of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q986305}} are termed '''Gyōkei''' (行啓) or '''Junkēi''' (巡啓). Combined outings with the emperor are called '''Gyōkōkei''' (行幸啓) or '''Junkōkei''' (巡幸啓). The term "Gyōkōkei" is commonly used to describe outings where both the emperor and empress are present.
When imperial family members return from an outing, it is referred to as '''Kankō''' (還幸), '''Kankei''' (還啓), or '''Kankōkei''' (還幸啓).
In the [[Nihon Shoki]], "Tennō Kō" (天皇幸) signifies the emperor's outing (Gyōkō), and "Tennō Shiji" (天皇至自) signifies his return (Kankō). Removing "Tennō" implies a royal or close relative's Gyōkō or Kankō. Participation in ceremonies during Gyōkō is termed '''Rinkō''' (臨幸).
For other members of the imperial family, outings are referred to as '''御成り''' (Onari), and their return is called '''Gokikan''' (御帰還). Temporary accommodations during Gyōkō are called '''{{ill|Angū|ja|行宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Angū|1=kinzai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626047}}''' (かりみや).<ref>Referenced in the Nihon Shoki, suggesting usage as early as the Asuka period.</ref>
== Historical Notable Gyōkō == === Notable Historical Examples === ; Emperor Go-Yōzei (107th Emperor) * {{ill|Jurakudai Gyōkō|ja|聚楽第行幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jurakudai Gyōkō|1=jurakuteigyoukou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22123524}} (April 14-18, 1588) ; Emperor Meiji (122nd Emperor) * Regional Tours: Conducted from 1868 (Meiji 1).<ref>{{Cite book |author=Toyama Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |year=2012 |title=御製とともに巡る富山県の聖蹟|page=27 |publisher=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}}</ref> ; Emperor Shōwa (124th Emperor) * [[Postwar Gyōkō|Postwar Tours]]: Conducted from 1946 to 1954.
== Related Terms == * {{ill|Miyuki|ja|御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki|1=gokou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16280183}}: Roads, parks, or places named after imperial outings.
{{translated page|ja|行幸|version=102597129|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:行幸]]
